summary: First iteration end-to-end testing of MailTrack (Gmail tracking dashboard + Chrome extension backend). Seeded MongoDB user, session, and a tracked_email. Created /app/backend/tests/test_mailtrack_api.py with 13 pytest tests covering auth, tracking pixel, emails, follow-ups, stats. 12/13 backend tests pass. Frontend landing/login/dashboard/settings/emails/follow-ups all verified loading correctly with auth cookie seeded; stats and ext_api_key display properly.

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error. Root cause: `await db.follow_ups.insert_one(doc)` mutates `doc` to add MongoDB `_id` (ObjectId), and the endpoint returns `{k: v for k, v in doc.items()}` which still contains _id, causing FastAPI JSON serialization to fail with `TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable`. Fix: pop `_id` before returning, or build response dict before insert_one. Same risk pattern would apply if /track/create or /auth/session were ever changed similarly (currently safe).

minor.issue: After insert_one, no _id strip needed because endpoint returns explicit dict — OK, but `doc` mutation pattern is fragile codebase-wide.

Stores expires_at as datetime object (not ISO string) while created_at is ISO string — inconsistent. get_current_user handles both, so works, but inconsistent with project rule 'datetime stored as ISO strings'.

integration_issues.issue: Because POST /api/follow-ups returns 500, any UI 'Create follow-up' action will fail. UI was not exercised for create (no creation form exposed on follow-ups page in DOM scan), but if/when wired it will break.

issues: Looks clean and on-brand. No issues.
All render correctly with proper spacing, contrast, and sidebar nav highlighting.

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: POST /api/follow-ups must strip MongoDB _id from response. Replace `return {k: v for k, v in doc.items()}` with explicit dict (excluding `_id`) or `doc.pop('_id', None)` before return.
Optional: standardize datetime persistence to ISO strings everywhere (expires_at in user_sessions is currently a datetime object).
Note: SSE /api/events/stream causes ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED occasionally in browser console — non-blocking but worth checking timeout/keepalive.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 326: `return {k: v for k, v in doc.items()}` returns a copy that still contains the post-insert `_id` key (ObjectId) — root cause of the 500.
server.py: insert_one mutating shared dict pattern is risky; prefer building response dict from a typed object first.
server.py is 431 lines, currently within size limits but growing — consider splitting into routers (auth/tracking/follow-ups) if it grows further.
All other endpoints correctly project `{'_id': 0}` on reads. Good.
ext_api_key uses secrets.token_urlsafe(24) — secure. Rotate endpoint works.
CORS allow_origins='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec; Starlette permits it but browsers may block credentialed cross-origin. Set explicit origins.
test_credentials: session_token=test_session_1777984514110, ext_api_key=test_ext_key_1777984514110, user_id=test-user-1777984514110, tracked_email_id=trk_1777984514142 (seeded in MongoDB test_database)
seed_data_creation: Inserted test user, user_session (7-day expiry), and one tracked_email (sent 5 days ago) into MongoDB test_database. See /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Seeded MongoDB credentials are persisted in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_mailtrack_api.py after main agent fixes the follow-ups _id leak. The fix is a 1-line change. Frontend works fully when session_token cookie is set on the preview domain.
rca of the issue: POST /api/follow-ups 500: motor's insert_one mutates the input dict to add `_id: ObjectId(...)`. The endpoint returns a dict-comprehension copy of that mutated doc, so ObjectId leaks into the JSON response and pydantic/Starlette's jsonable_encoder raises TypeError. Reproduce: POST /api/follow-ups with valid Bearer + tracked_email_id → 500. Mitigation: `doc.pop('_id', None)` before returning, or construct the response from FollowUpCreate + computed fields directly.
